evals.prompt: I've got a notes TUI app in Go using bubbletea v2 (charm.land imports). Right now the list, title input, and body textarea are all full-screen and I swap between them with a state uint. I want to add a persistent header at the top that shows 'NOTES' in a purple background, and a status bar at the bottom that shows the current mode (LIST / TITLE / BODY) and the relevant key hints for that mode. The header and status bar should stay visible regardless of which view is active, and everything should adapt if the terminal is resized.
evals.expected_output: Go code that: (1) adds width/height fields to the model and handles tea.WindowSizeMsg, (2) defines lipgloss styles for a header and status bar, (3) wraps all view rendering in a shell that uses JoinVertical to compose header + content + status bar, (4) uses correct charm.land/bubbletea/v2 and charm.land/lipgloss/v2 imports
assertions: Uses tea.WindowSizeMsg to store width and height on the model
Defines a header style with a background color using lipgloss.NewStyle()
Defines a status bar style using lipgloss.NewStyle()
Uses lipgloss.JoinVertical to compose header + body + status bar
Status bar content varies by current state (shows different text for listView vs titleView vs bodyView)
Sets Width(m.width) on header and/or status bar so they fill the terminal width
Uses correct v2 import paths (charm.land/bubbletea/v2 and charm.land/lipgloss/v2)
Body height accounts for header and status bar heights

evals.prompt: Working on a terminal app in Go with bubbletea v2 and lipgloss v2 (charm.land/* imports). I want a split-pane layout: a narrow left panel (about 25 chars wide) showing a list of servers using the bubbles list component, and a wider right panel showing details about the selected server. When I press Tab it should switch which panel has focus — the active one gets a rounded purple border, the inactive one gets a dim gray rounded border. Arrow keys should only affect the focused panel. Currently I just have an empty model struct.
evals.expected_output: Go code that creates a model with embedded list.Model and a viewport or custom detail renderer, handles Tab to toggle focus between panels, applies different border styles based on focus, routes arrow key input only to the focused component, and handles tea.WindowSizeMsg to size both panels correctly
assertions: Imports charm.land/bubbles/v2/list
Model has a focusedPane field (or equivalent) to track which pane is active
Defines two distinct border styles — one for focused (colored) and one for blurred (dim/gray)
Tab key toggles the active pane
Key events are only forwarded to the currently focused component
Uses lipgloss.JoinHorizontal to place the two panes side by side
Handles tea.WindowSizeMsg and calls list.SetSize (or equivalent) to resize components
Left panel has a fixed or constrained width around 25 characters

evals.prompt: I have a bubbletea v2 app and I want to add a confirmation modal. When the user presses 'd' on a list item, a small centered dialog should pop up that says 'Delete this note? (y/n)'. While the modal is open, the list behind it should still be visible but the keyboard input should only go to the modal. When they press y it should delete and close, n or esc should cancel. How do I structure this with my existing state machine approach (using uint const iota for states)?
evals.expected_output: Code showing how to add a confirmModal state to the iota, render the background view and overlay the modal using lipgloss.Place(), and route keyboard input exclusively to the modal handler when that state is active. Should include the lipgloss modal styles and the full Update() routing logic.
assertions: Adds a confirmModal (or similar) state to the existing state machine via iota
Uses lipgloss.Place() with lipgloss.Center alignment to center the modal
Modal has a border (lipgloss.RoundedBorder or similar)
The background list content is rendered underneath the modal (not blank)
Keyboard input in the confirmModal state only handles y/n/esc — other keys are not forwarded to the list
Pressing y triggers a delete action and returns to listView
Pressing n or esc cancels and returns to listView
Uses correct charm.land/lipgloss/v2 import
